Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix tooltip clipping — render via portal with fixed positioning

Tooltips now render via createPortal to document.body with position:fixed,
so they escape any parent overflow:hidden containers. Coordinates are
calculated from the icon's bounding rect with smart edge clamping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix four issues from code review before dry run deployment

1. config.live.toml: max_exchange_pct 0.40 → 0.55 (two exchanges
   need headroom above 50% to avoid pre-trade rejections)

2. executor: partial fill (leg B fails after leg A fills) now returns
   the ArbitragePosition instead of None. Orchestrator detects the
   one-legged position, sends emergency unwind alert, and attempts
   immediate close. Risk manager now sees the unhedged delta.

3. optimizer: yield_variance computed from actual rate history via
   _compute_spread_variance() instead of hardcoded 1e-6. Conservative
   1e-4 default when <6 observations. Kelly sizing now produces
   meaningful sizes instead of enormous values capped by hard limits.

4. risk: adjust_for_regime derives from _base_config (the config
   passed at construction) instead of hardcoded RiskConfig() defaults.
   Custom kelly_fraction/leverage survive regime toggling.

377 tests passing.

commit.message:
fix: always transition to complete screen when last exercise is done

Move store.setPhase('complete') before the null guard in finishLesson so
the UI always advances regardless of auth/data state. Previously, if user
was momentarily null (e.g. token refresh) the function returned early,
leaving the user stuck on the last exercise with a disabled CheckButton.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(validation): data type validation for NM, DT, TS, SI fields (#74)

* feat(validation): add data type validation for NM, DT, TS, SI fields (fixes #62)

Extends the validation engine to check field values against the data
types declared in the embedded HL7 v2.5.1 dictionary. Pure Rust char
checks — no new dependencies.

Covered types:
- NM: optional sign, digits, optional decimal point
- DT: YYYY[MM[DD]] — digits only, valid month/day ranges
- TS: YYYY[MM[DD[HH[MM[SS[.S+]]]]]][+/-HHMM] — strips frac/tz before validating date portion
- SI: non-negative integer (digits only)

Composite and table-dependent types (XPN, CX, ID, IS, ST…) are
intentionally skipped to avoid false positives.

Only the first field component is validated to avoid spurious errors on
multi-component fields whose raw value contains ^ separators.

Frontend: INVALID_DATATYPE warnings get a distinct blue badge
(.validation-seg.type) in the validation panel, and their segment is
included in the yellow fieldWarningSegs map so typical-segment badges
turn amber.

9 new unit tests added.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: Add HL7 error message test cases for NM, SI, and DT/TS data types.

* refactor: improve code formatting for readability in validation functions and tests.

* fix(ui): INVALID_DATATYPE warnings must not affect typical-segment badge colour

MISSING_FIELD turns a badge yellow because a required field is absent —
that is a structural defect. INVALID_DATATYPE is a content warning; the
segment and field are present, so the badge should remain blue (present).

The previous commit incorrectly included INVALID_DATATYPE in fieldWarningSegs,
which turned any segment with a type warning yellow. Removing it restores the
intended colour semantics:

  red    → MISSING_SEGMENT
  yellow → MISSING_FIELD only
  blue   → present (may still have INVALID_DATATYPE notices in the panel)
  grey   → absent, not required

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update all docs for data type validation, diff toggle, and validation filter

- CHANGELOG: merge duplicate Added sections; add validation status filter
  (#60), data type validation (#62), diff dynamic fields toggle (#61), and
  badge colour fix to the correct sections
- README: update Validation Engine bullet; add Validation Filter and updated
  Segment Diff bullets
- PROJECT_OVERVIEW: update parser pipeline step 4 to describe validate_data_types;
  update frontend key behaviours with three-tier badge colours, diff toggle,
  and validation filter
- STYLE_GUIDE: add Validation Badge Colour Semantics section documenting the
  red/amber/blue distinction and the rule that INVALID_DATATYPE must not affect
  typical-segment badge colours
- ROADMAP: add checked items for #60, #61, #62 to Milestone 3 task list

feat: Convergence Manifesto + AbsorptionRegistry contract

Convergence Manifesto: canonical philosophy document codifying:
  - Shapley fairness for all absorbed protocols
  - Attribution via ContributionDAG + PlaceholderEscrow
  - Lawson Fairness Floor (1% minimum for absorbed code)
  - Will's retroactive claim for 10 years of sacrifice

AbsorptionRegistry.sol: on-chain attribution for absorbed protocols
  - Records protocol name, source, license, developers
  - Links PlaceholderEscrow identities for reward claiming
  - Usage tracking per-contract for Shapley computation
  - Lawson Fairness Floor enforced at contract level

Built in a cave. With a box of scraps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add favicon.ico so Safari home page tab shows the lobster icon

Safari auto-requests /favicon.ico for the root domain regardless of
<link rel="icon"> tags. Cloudflare Pages was routing /favicon.ico to
index.html (SPA-style fallback), returning text/html — Safari silently
fell back to the generated "C" letter icon for the root URL.

Subpages (/episodes etc.) were unaffected because Safari uses
<link rel="icon"> directly for non-root paths.

Fix: add a proper ICO binary (two PNG images: 16x16 + 64x64) to the
repo root so Cloudflare serves image/x-icon at /favicon.ico.

Patch v1.3.3

feat: Phase 1 unified retrieval router — cross-system memory skill

New skill: memory-unified-recall
- Intent classification: TEMPORAL, SESSION, PATTERN, REFERENCE,
  ARCHITECTURE, RESEARCH, DEBUGGING, DECISION, DEFAULT
- Routes queries to Fusion Memory and/or Obsidian Vault based on intent
- Fusion first for decisions/temporal, Obsidian first for patterns/refs
- Parallel query both for research topics, merge results
- Bounded retrieval (max 3 calls per system), synthesize don't dump
- Output contract: unified_findings, sources, retrieval_log, confidence

commit.message:
Add /reflect skill for session-scoped learning extraction (#10)

* feat: add /compound-learnings skill for session-scoped learning extraction

Reviews the current conversation to extract corrections, preferences,
patterns, decisions, and pitfalls, then saves them as memories, rules,
or skills. Complements /deep-sleep (which processes past transcripts
in batch) by operating on the live conversation already in context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: rename /compound-learnings back to /reflect

Fits the cognitive activity naming paradigm established by /sleep
and /deep-sleep.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use semantic search for dedup in /reflect skill

Instead of listing files with ls and manually reading, leverage the
skill-router's own semantic search to find related existing content.
Each learning gets searched as a natural query — matches are either
skipped (already covered), updated (incomplete/misleading), or created
(no match).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: rewrite /reflect to properly leverage skill-router system

- Add "Context" section explaining the router's disclosure model so
  Claude executing the skill understands how types are surfaced
- Replace vague "type as natural query" dedup with concrete instructions
  to Glob/Read the router's actual scan paths and compare semantically
- Align classification table with router types and disclosure behavior
- Fix memory path encoding to match actual logic (/ → -, . → -)
- Add guidance on query quality, one-liner importance, and description
  as teaser for skills
- Add memory file format with ## headings and Triggers: lines

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: align /reflect with knowledge lifecycle from PR #13

- Use skill-router binary for dedup (same pattern as /sleep and
  /deep-sleep) instead of manual Glob/Read instructions
- Add full type taxonomy: workflow, tool-guidance, stop-rule,
  session-learning — not just memory/rule/skill
- Add 3+ correction threshold for rule classification, consistent
  with /deep-sleep
- Reference telemetry system and /sleep promotion/demotion lifecycle
- Add "Trust the lifecycle" guideline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
